name: Amma
description: Amma is the supreme creator god in Dogon mythology. A solitary, self-sufficient being, Amma existed before all else, residing in a cosmic egg of nothingness. The creation story begins with Amma's desire to create life. Amma first attempted to create beings through parthenogenesis, resulting in a flawed and rebellious jackal named Yurugu. Dissatisfied, Amma then created twins, Nommo, who were perfect androgynous beings representing balance and order. These Nommo descended to Earth, bringing culture and civilization to humanity. Amma is often depicted as a tiny grain of millet, symbolizing the potential for creation within the smallest of things. Amma's power lies in creation, order, and the maintenance of cosmic balance. While Amma is the ultimate creator, Amma remains distant from human affairs, and the Nommo act as intermediaries between the divine and the mortal realm. Amma is responsible for the creation of the sun, moon, stars, and the Earth itself. Amma's actions are not always easily understood by humans, emphasizing the deity's transcendence and unknowable nature. Amma represents the ultimate source of all existence and the foundation upon which the Dogon worldview is built. Amma is the ultimate origin of all things, both seen and unseen.
subtitle: The Supreme Creator God of the Dogon

keyMyths.title: The Creation of the Universe
keyMyths.description: Amma, alone in the cosmic egg, creates the universe through a series of actions and transformations. This process involves the creation of the sun, moon, stars, and Earth.

keyMyths.title: The Creation of the Nommo
keyMyths.description: Amma creates the Nommo, perfect androgynous twins, who bring order and civilization to Earth. They are seen as intermediaries between Amma and humanity.

keyMyths.title: The Creation of Yurugu
keyMyths.description: Amma's first attempt at creation results in Yurugu, a flawed and rebellious jackal. This highlights the importance of balance and order in Amma's creative process.

sources.author: Marcel Griaule
sources.description: A detailed account of Dogon cosmology and mythology based on conversations with a Dogon elder.
sources.text: A detailed account of Dogon cosmology and mythology based on conversations with a Dogon elder.

sources.author: Genevieve Calame-Griaule
sources.work: Ethnologie et langage: La parole chez les Dogon
sources.description: Explores the importance of language and symbolism in Dogon culture and mythology.
